== Gaza genocide in the first paragraph ==
{{atopg|result=Consensus found and implemented by [[User:إيان]], see comment at end of discussion. Archiving [[User:ScrubbedFalcon|ScrubbedFalcon]] ([[User talk:ScrubbedFalcon|talk]]) 23:12, 2 March 2026 (UTC)}}
Per [[MOS:OPEN]], which states that the first paragraph {{tq|should establish the context in which the topic is being considered by supplying the set of circumstances or facts that surround it|q=y}}, shouldn't [[Gaza genocide]] be mentioned and linked in the first paragraph? [[User:إيان|إيان]] ([[User talk:إيان|talk]]) 07:36, 9 January 2026 (UTC)

:'''Support''' per [[WP:NOTABLE]]. [[User:Achmad Rachmani|Achmad Rachmani]] ([[User talk:Achmad Rachmani|talk]]) 06:56, 11 January 2026 (UTC)
:'''Support''' per due context for establishing notability. This is one of the most notable/read child articles of this topic, alongside the invasion article and casualties of the war that are both in the opening paragraph, thus it can easily be inserted into the sentence about Palestinian casualties. To clarify before it becomes a discussion, I don't see this as even an NPOV-related proposal. This is about providing navigation for the reader to the prominent subtopics of a [[WP:SUMMARY|higher-level summary article]] (as we've always done with this page); likewise it's about establishing the notability for the topic itself (which is largely based on the range of child articles and their popularity). To prove this, see the comparison views for equally prominently referenced child articles in past few months.[https://pageviews.wmcloud.org/?project=en.wikipedia.org&platform=all-access&agent=user&redirects=0&range=latest-90&pages=Gaza_war|Gaza_genocide|Casualties_of_the_Gaza_war|October_7_attacks] The genocide article is currently sought after more so than [[October 7 attacks]] and [[Casualties of the Gaza war]], and based on past year (including previous October 7 title), it was in between Casualties and October 7 for views (~1.5m, ~2m, and ~2.3m).[https://pageviews.wmcloud.org/?project=en.wikipedia.org&platform=all-access&agent=user&redirects=0&range=last-year&pages=Gaza_war|Gaza_genocide|Casualties_of_the_Gaza_war|October_7_attacks|October_7_Hamas-led_attack_on_Israel] Granted that's just one metric/consideration, but it's the one I'm bringing to the table. [[User:CommunityNotesContributor|CNC]] ([[User talk:CommunityNotesContributor|talk]]) 10:33, 11 January 2026 (UTC)
:'''Oppose'''. Per MOS:FIRST we shouldn't {{tquote|overload the first sentence by describing everything notable about the subject.}} Let's keep the facts in the first sentence and the characterisations later in the lede. [[User:Alaexis|Alaexis]]<sub>[[User_talk:Alaexis|¿question?]]</sub> 20:30, 11 January 2026 (UTC)
::MOS:OPEN as proposed is about the opening paragraph, not the opening sentence. [[User:CommunityNotesContributor|CNC]] ([[User talk:CommunityNotesContributor|talk]]) 21:49, 11 January 2026 (UTC)
:'''Comment''' - I agree that the Gaza genocide is a highly notable aspect of the Gaza war & thus on significance alone, it would be more then reasonable for it to be mentioned earlier then the fourth paragraph. However, when considering the effect this would have on the lede's structure, I'm undecided on if this would be an improvement. More specifically, my concern is how existing content would be shifted to accommodate this change & if the lede would still read well overall. I'm open to supporting this proposal, but would first need to see how it'd be implemented. - [[User:Butterscotch Beluga|Butterscotch Beluga]] ([[User talk:Butterscotch Beluga|talk]]) 23:23, 11 January 2026 (UTC)
::There are currently over 70,000 recorded direct deaths (basically from blunt force trauma) to say nothing of the substantial indirect deaths—what other feature of the war could possibly be more important? For comparison, the full second sentence, containing 34 words, is dedicated to the October 7 attacks, which resulted in 1200 deaths.
::What is being proposed here is something as simple as, for example:
::{{textdiff|Since the start of the Israeli offensive that followed, over 70,000 Palestinians in Gaza have been killed, almost half of them women and children, and more than 171,000 injured.| Since the start of the Israeli offensive that followed, over 70,000 Palestinians in Gaza have been killed in the [[Gaza genocide]], almost half of them women and children, and more than 171,000 injured.}} [[User:إيان|إيان]] ([[User talk:إيان|talk]]) 15:26, 12 January 2026 (UTC)
:'''Support''' per CNC. The proposed text by إيان reads well, but the 70k figure should be updated to 71k in line with the latest RS, which are already reflected in the article. [[User:Paprikaiser|Paprikaiser]] ([[User talk:Paprikaiser|talk]]) 21:13, 19 January 2026 (UTC)
:I agree that it should be made part of the first paragraph. Justified by the notability rule. [[User:Josep a11|Josep a11]] ([[User talk:Josep a11|talk]]) 22:21, 19 January 2026 (UTC)
::'''Support''' (sorry, I forgot to include it) [[User:Josep a11|Josep a11]] ([[User talk:Josep a11|talk]]) 22:24, 19 January 2026 (UTC)
:'''Support''' - The proposed edit is concise and helps with reader's navigation. [[User:Jotamide|Jotamide]] ([[User talk:Jotamide|talk]]) 19:58, 21 January 2026 (UTC)
:[[User:Davefelmer|Davefelmer]], regarding your removals [https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Gaza_war&diff=prev&oldid=1333741465 here] and [https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Gaza_war&diff=prev&oldid=1333742606 here], there is a discussion here in case you'd like to participate. [[User:إيان|إيان]] ([[User talk:إيان|talk]]) 18:54, 22 January 2026 (UTC)
::{{u|إيان}}, please note [[MOS:FIRST]] and [[MOS:LEADCLUTTER]], before overloading the first sentence. While I think mentioning the genocide in the first paragraph is probably DUE, please note that the first sentence should {{Green|"should introduce the topic, and tell the nonspecialist reader what or who the subject is, and often when or where...should give a concise definition".}} In particular, please read {{green|"Do not overload the first sentence by describing everything notable about the subject. Instead, spread the relevant information out over the entire lead."}} For reasons of flow, I would suggest that the note about genocide come at the end of the current lead paragraph. Finally, please ensure there is no duplication when making major changes; in this case, you need to assess what to do with the fourth lead paragraph. I have established the structure of the lead section on two or three occasions over the course of the war; please let me know if you would like my assistance again. [[User:AirshipJungleman29|~~ AirshipJungleman29]] ([[User talk:AirshipJungleman29|talk]]) 19:12, 22 January 2026 (UTC)
:::I disagree with putting the genocide of 70,000+ people after the attack that killed ~1200. [[User:إيان|إيان]] ([[User talk:إيان|talk]]) 19:15, 22 January 2026 (UTC)
::::Sadly, the genocide of 70,000+ people did happen after the attack that killed ~1200. What is the purpose of the first paragraph? Is it a) to list events by their notoriety, highest to lowest, or is it b) to {{green|"briefly summarize the most important points covered in an article"}}, in a way {{Green|"accessible to as broad an audience as possible"}}? You will find it much easier to treat events with their full dignity if you make the article as accessible as possible: and the easiest way to make it accessible is to order things chronologically.  [[User:AirshipJungleman29|~~ AirshipJungleman29]] ([[User talk:AirshipJungleman29|talk]]) 19:28, 22 January 2026 (UTC)
:::::[[MOS:OPEN]] says nothing about using chronological order; instead, it says "The first paragraph should define or identify the topic with a neutral point of view." The genocide is the definitive aspect of Israel’s conduct with regard to the Gaza Strip. [[MOS:LEADREL]] says that "According to the policy on due weight, emphasis given to material should reflect its relative importance to the subject, according to published reliable sources." [[User:إيان|إيان]] ([[User talk:إيان|talk]]) 20:32, 22 January 2026 (UTC)
::::::You are talking about [[WP:NPOV]] when Israel have not been found guilty of genocide by any relevant international body. What is the basis for including it in the lead in a matter-of-fact way as if it is a definitively settled fact? It is distinctly not neutral and not [[WP:DUE]]. We can move up the fourth paragraph on it into the back of the first but I don't see why it is particularly necessary. [[User:Davefelmer|Davefelmer]] ([[User talk:Davefelmer|talk]]) 03:20, 23 January 2026 (UTC)
::::::{{green|"The genocide is the definitive aspect of Israel’s conduct with regard to the Gaza Strip."}} That might very well be the case. Please feel free to include the genocide in the first sentence of your forthcoming article [[Israel's conduct with regard to the Gaza Strip]]. Now, may we discuss the first paragraph of the article [[Gaza war]]? The definitive events of a "[[war]]" are typically those relating to the military—hence why, per [[WP:DUE|central content policy]], 7,800 words of the article discuss military events, compared with less than 1,000 in the "War crimes and genocide subsection". You may find potential comparison articles at [[Rwandan Civil War]], [[War in Darfur]], [[Bangladesh Liberation War]], or [[Second Italo-Senussi War]]. [[User:AirshipJungleman29|~~ AirshipJungleman29]] ([[User talk:AirshipJungleman29|talk]]) 19:29, 23 January 2026 (UTC)
:::::::{{tq|Please feel free to include the genocide in the first sentence of your forthcoming article Israel's conduct with regard to the Gaza Strip. Now, may we discuss the first paragraph of the article Gaza war?|q=y}} Not sure what this is meant to achieve for you, but here's a reminder for all of us to maintain civility in this sensitive topic area. [[User:إيان|إيان]] ([[User talk:إيان|talk]]) 19:39, 1 February 2026 (UTC)
::::::::Thank you for the reminder, but I have found this discussion quite civil. I am also a little unclear on what the highlighted sentences are meant to achieve for me; if you figure it out, please let me know. [[User:AirshipJungleman29|~~ AirshipJungleman29]] ([[User talk:AirshipJungleman29|talk]]) 19:04, 5 February 2026 (UTC)
:'''Support''' <ins>per [[WP:NOTABLE]]</ins> with conditions. I think it should be in the first paragraph but just not very first sentence. It's significant enough to warrant easy access for the link in opening paragraph. <Del>After adding the link, I also believe it’s important to mention that <span style="color:green;">casualties are estimated at about 15 out of every 16 being civilians, making Gaza one of the deadliest conflicts for civilians in recent decades.</span>[https://www.theguardian.com/world/2025/sep/19/civilians-made-up-15-of-every-16-people-israel-killed-in-gaza-since-march-data-suggests]</del> <ins>I propose adding the link in the final word of this statement- <span style="color:green;">Since the start of the Israeli offensive that followed, over <del>80,000</del><ins>100,000</ins> Palestinians in Gaza have been killed, with scholars estimating that approximately 27% of those killed are children under the age of 15 and 24% are women, a demographic distribution which the researchers note corresponds to patterns the United Nations has observed in past genocides, and a number of scholar and international organizations have increasingly described the situation as being a [[Gaza genocide|genocide]].[https://www.zeit.de/politik/ausland/2025-11/kriegstote-gazastreifen-opferzahlen-schaetzung]</span></ins> [[User:JaredMcKenzie|JaredMcKenzie]] ([[User talk:JaredMcKenzie|talk]]) 18:45, 23 January 2026 (UTC)
::Do you have any sources for children between the ages of 15 and 18? Per several [[WP:RS]], teenage children are also recruited to Palestinian militant groups. [[User:Pachu Kannan|Pachu Kannan]] ([[User talk:Pachu Kannan|talk]]) 09:20, 24 January 2026 (UTC)
:::Clearly total children (under 18) will be larger than 27%. But that Max Planck study doesn't explicitly mention a group under 18, but just under age 15. If you do the math - (male and females between the ages 15 to 18), plus the men over age 18 will make up that remaining 49 percent. Source doesn't specify how much of this 49 percent is fighters. But like every other country, the majority of men aren't soldiers. If you need a source to know how many dead are civilians - a different group - independent conflict tracker Acled that tracks civillian and Hamas deaths, estimates 15 of every 16 Palestinians killed are civilians.[https://www.theguardian.com/world/2025/sep/19/civilians-made-up-15-of-every-16-people-israel-killed-in-gaza-since-march-data-suggests] [[User:JaredMcKenzie|JaredMcKenzie]] ([[User talk:JaredMcKenzie|talk]]) 09:38, 24 January 2026 (UTC)
:'''Comment''': Support for this is virtually unanimous, yet it wasn't there yet. So I [https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Gaza_war&diff=prev&oldid=1337235413 tried this]. [[User:إيان|إيان]] ([[User talk:إيان|talk]]) 06:34, 8 February 2026 (UTC)
::'''Oppose''' for chronological reasons and since it would make much more sense to put it in the third paragraph that details the humanitarian crisis and destruction. I have [https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Gaza_war&diff=prev&oldid=1337243598 moved it back] to the end of the first paragraph following you edit.
::Best, [[User:NorthernWinds|NorthernWinds]] ([[User talk:NorthernWinds|talk]]) 08:20, 8 February 2026 (UTC)
:::I'm a bit confused, @[[User:NorthernWinds|NorthernWinds]] -- if you think it makes {{tq|much more sense to put it in the third paragraph}}, why did you put it at the end of the first paragraph? [[User:Coining|Coining]] ([[User talk:Coining|talk]]) 14:17, 8 February 2026 (UTC)
::::@[[User:Coining|Coining]] Due to the support an inclusion in the first paragraph has (6-2 when I wrote this comment, 6-3 now after you've voiced your opinion). Feel free to put it back where it was if you think the discussion is not ripe yet [[User:NorthernWinds|NorthernWinds]] ([[User talk:NorthernWinds|talk]]) 14:41, 8 February 2026 (UTC)
:::::@[[User:Coining|Coining]] note I moved it back from where Ian put it ([https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Gaza_war&diff=prev&oldid=1337235413 he put it in the first sentence]). [[User:NorthernWinds|NorthernWinds]] ([[User talk:NorthernWinds|talk]]) 14:48, 8 February 2026 (UTC)
:'''Oppose''' until someone explains to me why [[Talk:Gaza_genocide/Archive_12#RfC_on_first_sentence|an RfC on a different Wikipedia article discussing the first sentence of that article]] is binding on this article. [[User:Coining|Coining]] ([[User talk:Coining|talk]]) 14:22, 8 February 2026 (UTC)
::Not sure I follow your reasoning. Why would someone arguing that it is or it is not binding on this article be pertinent to the question asked by إيان about this article? Your !vote seems to be based on the absence of something that isn't necessary to answer the question asked. [[User:Sean.hoyland|Sean.hoyland]] ([[User talk:Sean.hoyland|talk]]) 16:05, 8 February 2026 (UTC)
:::Well, absent that RfC, would it be appropriate to [https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Gaza_war&diff=prev&oldid=1337235413 state in Wikivoice] that a genocide has occurred? [[User:Coining|Coining]] ([[User talk:Coining|talk]]) 18:04, 8 February 2026 (UTC)
::::No need for wikivoice when you can just give attribution. Due to the political nature, we can never expect 100 percent consensus as there will always be deniers. Tho we shouldn't do false balance. When majority of leading genocide experts [https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/cde3eyzdr63o], and top reputable human rights bodies conclude that it's a genocide.[https://www.amnesty.org.au/amnesty-concludes-israel-genocide-in-gaza/] Then trying to conflate that a fringe group of politically motivated/state-aligned denial as an equal equivalence - will be what's wrong. That is not neutrality but distortion per WP:UNDUE. On Wiki, one can write that; <span style="color:green;">Most independent human-rights bodies, and many leading genocide and international law scholars allege that Israel's actions in Gaza plausibly constitute genocide, or at minimum meet multiple elements of the Genocide Convention.</span> [[User:JaredMcKenzie|JaredMcKenzie]] ([[User talk:JaredMcKenzie|talk]]) 00:37, 9 February 2026 (UTC)
:::::So we're agreed that [https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Gaza_war&diff=prev&oldid=1337235413 yesterday's attempt] to implement this discussion thread was inappropriate because it used Wikivoice? [[User:Coining|Coining]] ([[User talk:Coining|talk]]) 03:10, 9 February 2026 (UTC)
::::::Why was it {{tq|inappropriate|q=y}}? [[User:إيان|إيان]] ([[User talk:إيان|talk]]) 03:18, 9 February 2026 (UTC)

There are venues for addressing conduct concerns, article talk pages are for discussions of content. {{pb}} On that note, there are two parts to the content cited to The Lancet: the conclusion that the GMH death toll is undercounted and the note that indirect deaths – those resulting from lack of access to food and clean water, loss of healthcare (particularly for women's health issues, such as pregnancy complications), spread of infectious diseases, etc – when accounted for increase the death toll substantially. The proposition that basic facts should be subjected to a lower bar for lede inclusion than analyses or speculations may be reasonable, but that concern only applies to the former statement. The latter statement ''is'' a basic fact presented in the most conservative language available: 'a potentially higher death toll'. In modern conflicts, the bulk of deaths are 'indirect'. {{pb}} For the former statement, there is additional coverage of the study in independent media and it is cited in later academic studies (both types presented as supporting citations with The Lancet content). DUE weight is measured by the prevalence of viewpoints in reliable sources, not in the Wikipedia article: '... in proportion to the prominence of each viewpoint in ''those sources'''. LEADFOLLOWSBODY is a separate subject that informs you that the content in the lede promises fuller coverage in the body – which we demonstrably have. Thus I am not convinced that an issue exists from the standpoint of either P&G. Others may conclude differently and are welcome to opine in this discussion. {{pb}} Side-correction: I stated that the death toll for Palestinians is not repeated in prose. That's half-true. The figure in the lede is not present in the body. The body contains the figure of '73,158+ Palestinians' accounting for both Gaza and the West Bank; while the lede only reports the figure for Gaza.
